goal: 让 mainline hub 更适合人类阅读和使用

summary.title: Human-friendly Hub dashboard and review queue
summary.what: Reworked Hub v1 from a table-first static export into a human-oriented dashboard. The exported model now includes dashboard rollups, focus intents, status counts, and hot files. The index page surfaces open work, review queue, risk-bearing intents, hot files, and a prioritized attention list before the raw recent table. Added a dedicated review.html page for proposed intents and reshaped intent detail pages into scannable user goal / what / why / decisions blocks. Updated the stylesheet to a cleaner application layout with responsive card and grid treatments.
summary.why: The CLI remains the agent-oriented interface, while Hub should help human readers quickly decide where to look: current open work, proposed changes needing review, risky sealed work, and files with dense intent history. The previous Hub v1 proved the export path, but the landing page still read like a raw log/table dump. This change keeps the static, no-JS v1 architecture from int_41382daf while making the first screen answer the user's practical questions faster.
summary.user_goal: Make mainline hub easier for humans to read and use, with better functionality and visual presentation than the current simple export.

decisions.chose: Add HubDashboard to HubModel instead of deriving all homepage groupings in templates.
decisions.rationale: HubModel is the durable v1/v2 contract from int_41382daf. Keeping rollups in the model makes the human-first landing view testable, JSON-visible, and reusable by a future hosted Hub API.

decisions.chose: Add a static review.html page for proposed intents and link the dashboard metric to it.
decisions.rationale: A human-facing Hub should provide a direct path from 'what needs attention' to the exact proposed work, rather than showing an inert count in the dashboard.

decisions.chose: Keep pure server-rendered static HTML/CSS with no JavaScript or framework.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the throwaway/static Hub v1 constraint from int_41382daf and avoids turning a validation prototype into a client app.

decisions.chose: Promote user goal / what / why / decisions into card-like reading blocks before secondary evidence.
decisions.rationale: Humans usually open a detail page to understand intent and rationale first; raw evidence and CLI commands remain available lower on the page.

decisions.chose: Keep files/open/risks routes from int_3407b565 and add review as a sibling route instead of replacing existing pages.
decisions.rationale: This preserves recently added Hub v1 review fixes while improving the landing flow around them.

rejected.reason: Useful later, but static Hub v1 can gain a clearer human workflow without adding JavaScript or a new interaction model.

rejected.alternative: Replace the recent intents table entirely with cards.
rejected.reason: The table remains useful as a dense audit view; the dashboard now handles first-screen orientation and the table stays as the detailed recent list.

rejected.alternative: Make Review queue include risk-heavy merged work too.
rejected.reason: Risk-heavy work already has risks.html. Keeping review.html scoped to proposed intents gives it a clear operational meaning.

risks: data/intents.json now includes a dashboard object. JSON consumers that validate the HubModel shape strictly and reject unknown fields could need an update, even though existing Go consumers should tolerate additive fields.

The dashboard attention ordering is heuristic: proposed intents first, then risky intents, then recent merged work. A future team with different review conventions may need configurable prioritization if this becomes a hosted Hub surface.
followups: Dogfood the new dashboard with a larger intent history and decide whether a no-JS text filter/search is still needed.
If Hub v2 proceeds, carry HubDashboard into the API DTO and decide whether focus ordering should become configurable per team.

architectural_claims: Hub remains a static no-JS export; renderer complexity stays in Go templates and CSS.
HubDashboard is part of the HubModel JSON contract and is derived from existing intent, file, actor, risk, and open-work indexes.
Review queue is a static sibling page scoped to proposed intents, not a client-side filter over the recent table.
behavioral_changes: `mainline hub export` now emits review.html in addition to the existing index/open/files/risks/graph/detail pages.
index.html now opens as a human dashboard with summary metrics, open work link, attention list, hot files, and the recent intents table below.
intent detail pages render user goal, what changed, why, and decisions as scannable blocks instead of plain section text.
data/intents.json includes dashboard totals, status counts, focus rows, and hot files.

data_model_changes.name: HubDashboard
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Existing HubModel fields remain; dashboard is a new top-level JSON object.

security_implications: No new network IO, forms, JavaScript, permissions, or user-data transmission.
New template output is still rendered through html/template auto-escaping.
